conversation_history.assistant: Eyes open. Salt wind, distant bells. Body settles, mind awake, path ahead clear.

Darkness. Then pain—cold air burns first breath. Above, fractured stone drips orange light. Distant picks strike rhythm like heartbeat. Hands rough as ore grip you, lift toward glow. Taste dust and copper. Eyes open: veins of crystal pulse in walls, matching faint light under your skin. Sound: stone groaning, water far below. Hunger begins.

Klein opens his eyes to a pale dawn filtering through ash-grey clouds, the smell of forge smoke and cold stone sharp in his nostrils. Distant clang of training swords echoes across the courtyard, and the warm pulse of his own ember veins flickers under his skin as he pushes himself up from the worn flagstones. A raven perched on the barracks roof cocks its head, watching him with an unreadable glint.
Salt air fills lungs. Gulls cry. Waves lap wood.
Eyes open to salt wind and distant gulls. Ear fills with hammer, laughter, current of harbor. Body remembers birth, curious heart ready to learn Vorath.

Sky ash falls. Grey flakes dance on salt wind. Ash respawns from Last Ember Ruin weekly. Never settles fully.

Waves lap hull wood. Ropes creak against masts. Distant hammers strike hot iron. Laughter echoes across salt water.

Wet planks soak through cloth. Cold bites skin. Storm passed. Drift-echo released cradle here.

Gulls wheel overhead. Ash coats tongue. Taste copper, brine, smoke.

Harbor spreads before eyes. Ships bob on grey water. Galecrest buildings cling to cliffs. Lights flicker in windows.

Empty places where friends stood. Voices gone. Names written in ash now.

Ship lists to one side. Mast snaps took half our sailing. Sail tore in storm-wrath. What remains fits in two hands.

Soul-stuff shifts around. Cannot create or destroy. Only moves or consumes. Some consumed now. Know this. Feel hollow where warmth lived.

Sun lowers toward ash-clouds. Harbor activity slows. Time passes.

Defeat tastes ash. Brine. Copper.
